questions.question: What is true about the concept of peer-to-peer networks?

answers.text: Peer-to-peer applications allow their users to control how many node connections should be searched for or allowed at the same time.
A peer-to-peer network is a centralized communication model that can establish communication to all other nodes.
The computers of some users are both providers and consumers of resources.

questions.question: What are advantages of peer-to-peer networks?
What is true about distributed ledger technology?

answers.text: Distributed ledger technologies slow down transactions by integrating a central instance.
Distributed ledgers have the tendency to raise costs per transaction.
Distributed ledger technology creates a system that is more difficult to manipulate or successfully attack.
Distributed ledgers have neither a central data store nor centralized management functions.

questions.question: What does SPOF mean in the context of network architectures?
What are the primary advantages of decentralized systems compared to centralized ones?
Explain distributed ledger technology in a few words.

answers.text: Decentralized data storage and transmission across networked computers.

questions.question: List potential benefits of distributed ledger technology.

answers.text: Faster transactions, reduced costs, enhanced security, and improved transparency.

questions.question: What is a characteristic of peer-to-peer (P2P) networks?

answers.text: They rely on a central coordination point.
They have strict user control over network parameters.
Each node has the same capabilities and can establish communication with all other nodes.

questions.question: What are the disadvantages of distributed systems compared to central individual computers?
What is a primary characteristic of blockchain technology?

answers.text: It relies on a centralized database.
It has a single point of failure.
It enables distributed and tamper-proof data management.

questions.question: Describe the primary characteristics of blockchain or distributed ledger technology.

answers.text: Decentralized, tamper-proof data storage and transmission.

questions.question: The actor who solves the hash puzzle represents the majority and is allowed to propose a transaction for posting to the blockchain. What is this mechanism called?
Which of the following are valid standard consensus methods?
What type of blockchain requires explicit access authorization?
What are the characteristics of cryptocurrency transactions?

answers.text: They can be carried out without a digital wallet.
They require physical representation of currency.
They use strongly encrypted hash codes.

questions.question: What is the consequence of changing a block's information in a blockchain?

answers.text: Breaks the hash sequence, disrupting consensus.

questions.question: What happens if consensus is not reached in a blockchain network?

answers.text: No new blocks are added until the issue is resolved.

questions.question: What is primarily used to manage blockchain transaction data and status?
Blockchain technology is seen as a means to improve which of the following?
Why do many organizations prefer private blockchain systems?
What fundamental aspect does blockchain provide for business transactions?
What primary goal does the use of public/private key pairs in blockchain technology serve?
What does the Byzantine Generals Problem illustrate in the context of blockchain?

answers.text: The need for simultaneous actions in a transaction
The challenges of achieving consensus among distributed peers
The importance of encryption in communications

questions.question: Why does the Byzantine Generals Problem relate to blockchain technology?

answers.text: It demonstrates the difficulty of achieving consensus in a decentralized environment, especially when there is a possibility of betrayal or misinformation among peers.

questions.question: How is the immutability of the blockchain ledger achieved?

answers.text: The ledger is tamper-proof, ensuring no participant can alter entries once they are saved, and it is managed to be immune to attacks.

questions.question: Which aspects are critical to maintaining integrity in blockchain systems?

answers.text: Knowledge of the number of peers
Knowledge of the trustworthiness of peers

questions.question: What are the primary threats to integrity in peer-to-peer blockchain systems?
What is essential for proof of ownership in blockchain technology?

answers.text: Assignment of the owner to the object only
Identification of the owner and the object

questions.question: How does blockchain prevent the double spending of digital assets?

answers.text: By synchronizing ledgers to update all transactions in real time, preventing unauthorized repeats.

questions.question: What does double spending exploit in blockchain systems?

answers.text: The physical nature of digital assets
The time delay in updating and synchronizing transaction information

questions.question: What are key design features of ledgers in blockchain for untrusted environments?

answers.text: Ledgers are immutable and distributed, ensuring secure and shared transaction histories.

questions.question: Which tasks must blockchain software manage in a peer-to-peer environment?

answers.text: Enhancing the speed of transaction verification

questions.question: What does semantic correctness in transaction processing focus on?

answers.text: The time a transaction is executed.
The intended effect of transaction data.
The fee payable for executing a transaction.

questions.question: Which of the following are criteria for adding transaction data to the blockchain?
What must be verified to ensure the correct transfer of ownership in blockchain transactions?
What is the tree structure that groups many pieces of data and makes them accessible through a single hash reference called?
Which statements about hash values are correct?

answers.text: Hashing is used to compare data by comparing its content piece by piece.
If all cryptographic hash values differ, all the data under consideration are also different.
If two or more cryptographic hash values are identical, their corresponding input data is also identical.
Any manipulation of the underlying data can be detected because the timestamp of the hash value differs.

questions.question: Which statements about the role of cryptographic hash functions in blockchain security are true?

answers.text: They allow reverse engineering of the original data.
They ensure no two different data pieces can have the same hash value.
They enable quick creation of hash values from any type of data.
They predict the hash value based on the input data.

questions.question: What is the purpose of using cryptographic hash functions for data security in blockchains?

answers.text: To ensure data integrity and detect any changes.

questions.question: What is the primary function of hash references in maintaining data integrity?

answers.text: To ensure that referenced data has not been altered
To increase the data storage capacity

questions.question: Why are hash references considered crucial for detecting data alterations in blockchain?

answers.text: They validate that the data they point to has not been modified; any change invalidates the reference, signaling an alteration.

questions.question: What challenges does the requirement of a nonce introduce in solving hash puzzles?

answers.text: Finding the correct nonce requires trial and error, guessing until the hash value of the combined data meets the set difficulty restrictions.

questions.question: How do corrupted hash references indicate data integrity issues?

answers.text: They suggest data has been altered after the reference was created.
They have no impact on data integrity.

questions.question: Which properties of cryptographic hash functions are essential for blockchain security?
What is the significance of the head of the chain in a dynamic record structure?

answers.text: It is irrelevant to the structure.
It references the most recently added record.
It encrypts the entire data chain.

questions.question: What is the primary function of hash references in blockchain technology?
Describe the role of hash puzzles in blockchain.
Explain the significance of Merkle trees.
Which qualities describe an encryption method based on the pairwise use of a private key and a public key?

answers.text: Being called a public key method
Being called a private key method

questions.question: What distinguishes asymmetric cryptography from symmetric cryptography?

answers.text: It uses the same key for encryption and decryption.
It relies on a single key for encryption and decryption.
It utilizes two complementary keys for encryption and decryption.
It employs complex algorithms for data transmission.

questions.question: What are the primary functions of public and private keys in asymmetric cryptography?

answers.text: Identifying account owners and decrypting messages.
Encrypting messages and ensuring data integrity.
Creating digital signatures and encrypting data.

questions.question: Describe the main steps of creating a digital signature in asymmetric cryptography.
Explain briefly how digital signatures verify the authenticity of messages.

answers.text: Create hash, decrypt signature, compare hashes.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a private key in asymmetric cryptography?
What is the significance of creating a digital signature for a transaction in blockchain?

answers.text: Identifying the recipient of the transaction.
Verifying the authenticity of the transaction.

questions.question: What is the role of the public key in secure communication using asymmetric cryptography?
How does asymmetric cryptography ensure ownership transfer in blockchain transactions?
How does asymmetric cryptography ensure secure communication between parties?

answers.text: By using the same key for encryption and decryption.
By encrypting messages with a private key.
By ensuring only the owner of the private key can decrypt encrypted text.

questions.question: Describe the consequences of modifying a transaction in the blockchain.
What happens if a hash reference within a block header is modified?

answers.text: Only the block header becomes invalid.
Only the referenced transaction becomes invalid.

questions.question: What is the impact of hash reference changes on the integrity of the Merkle tree?

answers.text: The root of the Merkle tree becomes invalid.
Only the affected branch of the Merkle tree is affected.
The Merkle tree structure remains unaffected.
The blockchain data structure becomes invalid.

questions.question: What happens when a transaction is replaced within the blockchain?

answers.text: The entire Merkle tree structure is invalidated.
Only the replaced transaction becomes invalid.
The block containing the replaced transaction becomes invalid.

questions.question: Explain the process of ensuring data integrity when modifying transactions.

answers.text: Update all hash references in sequence.

questions.question: What is the primary challenge in maintaining data integrity in blockchain?
How does the blockchain detect and prevent transaction manipulation through hash reference renewal?

answers.text: By renewing all hash references affected by changes.

questions.question: Which types of correctness are checked by validators after an actor proposes a transaction for posting?
What is the remuneration for solving the blockchain puzzle called?
What is the joint confirmation of exchange actions by all actors in a peer-to-peer blockchain network called?
What concept describes proving trustworthiness by doing something expensive and difficult?
What is the role of validators in a blockchain?

answers.text: To check the correctness of transactions

questions.question: Which consensus mechanism combines Proof of Work (PoW) with Proof of Stake (PoS)?
What is one way to punish actors who compromise the integrity of the blockchain?

answers.text: Reclaiming the reward for invalid blocks
Increasing their chances of being selected

questions.question: What characteristic distinguishes Proof of Importance (PoI) from Proof of Stake (PoS)?
Explain the concept of consensus in a blockchain.

answers.text: Agreement among network participants on the validity of transactions.

questions.question: What is the main drawback of the Proof of Work (PoW) consensus mechanism?

answers.text: High energy consumption and computational intensity.

questions.question: Who decides which branch of a blockchain to expand when there is a fork?

answers.text: The actor who first solves the puzzle of a new block.

questions.question: Which criteria are used to determine the valid transaction sequence in a blockchain?

answers.text: The criterion of the longest chain
The criterion of the heaviest chain

questions.question: What determines which branch of a blockchain is expanded when there is a fork?
What happens to orphaned blocks in a blockchain?

answers.text: They are processed to become part of the authorized history
They are abandoned by the actors

questions.question: How does the blockchain ensure resistance against manipulation?

answers.text: By majority control of computing power

questions.question: How are orphaned blocks handled in a blockchain?

answers.text: They are abandoned by the actors
They are processed to become part of the authorized history
They are used to extend the blockchain

questions.question: What are the consequences of selecting a chain as the authoritative version of transaction data?
What is the purpose of selecting the longest or heaviest chain?

answers.text: To determine the authoritative version of the transaction history.

questions.question: What determines which block is added to a blockchain when conflicting branches exist?
What is a smart contract?

answers.text: A smart contract is a computer protocol that determines interactions between actors in any type of blockchain.

questions.question: What is the core function of a smart contract?
What distinguishes public and private blockchains?
What distinguishes public and private blockchains?
Which design parameters should be considered when developing blockchain applications?

answers.text: Proof of Work vs. Proof of Stake

questions.question: Which approaches are used to balance security and speed in blockchain systems?
Which consensus mechanism is suitable for private blockchains with a central authority?
What is the role of the private key in blockchain security?
What poses a challenge to blockchain scalability?
How does a smart contract differ from a traditional legal contract?
What are the challenges of ensuring data privacy in blockchains?
What is the role of a private key in blockchain security?
The blockchain trilemma includes security and decentralization. What is the third aspect?
What instrument stores the money handled by a smart-contract microtransaction?
How can smart contracts benefit the healthcare industry?

answers.text: By preventing incorrect payments and fraud

questions.question: What challenges are associated with the consensus mechanism in blockchain design?
How can blockchain technology enhance transparency and efficiency in supply chains?

answers.text: By providing secure and transparent tracking of supply chain data among all stakeholders.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of using blockchain in the automotive industry?

answers.text: Storing easily accessible information about vehicle maintenance and ownership history

questions.question: What practical considerations are important in blockchain integration?

answers.text: Striving for interoperability with existing systems

questions.question: How do different consensus mechanisms affect blockchain performance?

answers.text: Proof of Stake (PoS) enables faster transactions at the expense of security
Proof of Authority (PoA) ensures decentralization with slower transaction speeds
Proof of Work (PoW) offers security but sacrifices scalability
Proof of Honesty (PoH) provides higher scalability but lower transaction speeds

questions.question: What are the challenges associated with integrating blockchain into current company systems?

answers.text: Ensuring interoperability and compliance with legal frameworks while maintaining data integrity.

questions.question: What type of transactions can smart contracts enable?

answers.text: Microtransactions for different types of billing

questions.question: Which parameter presents a challenge in the design of blockchain applications?
How can blockchain technology empower workforce management?

answers.text: By using smart contracts to automate payroll, benefits, and performance incentives, improving transparency and efficiency.
